hard.account_name: PT. Mitra Pinasthika Mustika Rent (MPM Rent)
hard.next_step: Initial meeting and Demo on 26th June with Pak Andianto and his team. Went well, impressed with the intake and how Bhasa Indonesia can be used for intake, Next steps to share high level pricing for internal approvals
hard.ais_why: AI Leadership Commitment 6.5; Digital Transformation Maturity 7.5; Procurement Complexity 8.0; Enterprise Scale 7.5; Likelihood to Invest in AI Procurement 6.8; Overall AIS 7.3/10 (Moderately attractive prospect).

north_star_verdict.headline: First demo on 26 June landed well on intake and Bahasa Indonesia, but the high-level pricing Pak Andianto needs for internal approvals has not gone out and there has been no buyer inbound in about 14 days, so an early deal is losing momentum before the budget owner is even mapped.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 11 June 2026, qualified 30 June 2026, first demo 26 June 2026 (~10 days before the 6 July sweep). Close date 31 December 2026 is 178 days out, credible for an APAC enterprise cycle. This is not a date problem; it is a momentum problem: the promised pricing is undelivered and the opportunity-health flags on 30 June record no buyer inbound in 14 days, which is one stalled action plus thinning engagement, so the read is Slowing, not Close Date Risk.
evidence: 26 June 2026 demo went well; buyer impressed with intake and how Bahasa Indonesia can be used for intake (source: Next_Step__c, 26 June 2026).
Pak Andianto asked for high-level pricing to route through internal approvals, a Zycus-specific next step, but that pricing is not yet delivered (source: Next_Step__c, 26 June 2026).

No economic buyer or decision maker mapped; Pak Andianto needing internal approvals means he does not control the $150k decision himself (source: OpportunityContactRole empty; Next_Step__c, 26 June 2026).
Single-threaded: only Pak Andianto and his team are named; no senior sponsor engaged (source: Next_Step__c, 26 June 2026; opportunity contact roster empty).
No competitor named in any field, task, or call; sole-source vs quiet multi-vendor evaluation is unknown (source: Competitors__c blank).
Opportunity-health flags on 30 June note no inbound in 14 days and 10 days without outbound (source: SF tasks, 30 June 2026).

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists in any source read this sweep. The 26 June demo focused on intake and Bahasa Indonesia request capture, which points to a qualitative pain around manual, English-only procurement request handling and user adoption, but no cost, cycle-time, headcount, error-rate, or savings figure has been stated (source: Next_Step__c, 26 June 2026). Quantifying the value case to justify the $150k and support the internal approval Pak Andianto referenced is the required move.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified. The only named contact, Pak Andianto, explicitly requested pricing 'for internal approvals', which shows he does not personally control the commercial decision on this $150k deal (source: Next_Step__c, 26 June 2026). There are no opportunity contact roles, so the approver (a CFO, CEO, or division head at MPM Rent) is entirely unmapped. Until the budget owner is identified and reached, pricing and ROI conversations cannot land with the person who signs, and the deal risks stalling at the procurement level.

decision_criteria.narrative: Two criteria are visible from the demo feedback: intake/request-management capability and Bahasa Indonesia local-language support for user adoption, both of which impressed the buyer on 26 June (source: Next_Step__c, 26 June 2026). The product scope (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin, iRisk) implies a broader procurement-transformation evaluation, and the AIS read (7.3 Strong) signals AI appetite, but no formal RFI/RFP criteria, weighted scorecard, or structured evaluation has been documented (source: Products__c; AIS_Score__c 7.3). Confirming the full evaluation criteria and whether a formal comparison is running is the move.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer referenced 'internal approvals', confirming a multi-step internal process exists, but its steps, approvers, gates, and timeline are unknown (source: Next_Step__c, 26 June 2026). The activity trail this sweep is only procurement-led with no escalation to finance, legal, or senior stakeholders, and no buyer-stated decision date. Mapping the approval chain and the timeline before pricing goes out, so it does not disappear into an undefined approval loop, is the required next step.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics have surfaced: no MSA, Order Form, SOW, procurement channel, InfoSec review, or ERP-integration scoping is mentioned in any source (source: no paper-process field or call evidence this sweep). The deal is still pre-commercial and awaiting pricing, so paper process is not yet a blocker, but it is entirely unmapped. Given the Indonesian geography, clarifying whether contracting runs direct or via a local entity/partner should happen before commercial negotiation begins.
sources: No paper-process evidence found this sweep

identify_pain.narrative: Qualitative pain is confirmed but unquantified and without a named owner. The buyer was 'impressed with the intake and how Bhasa Indonesia can be used for intake' (source: Next_Step__c, 26 June 2026), which implies current-state pain around manual procurement request handling and low user adoption due to language. No cost of the pain (FTE hours, delay, error rate), pain owner, or compelling event (contract expiry, audit, growth mandate) has been articulated. Confirming and quantifying the pain, and naming its owner, is the move.

champion.narrative: Pak Andianto is a developing champion: he hosted the 26 June demo with his team, reacted positively to intake and Bahasa Indonesia, and pulled the deal forward by requesting pricing for internal approvals (source: Next_Step__c, 26 June 2026). What has developed is genuine product enthusiasm and an intent to advance internally; what is still missing is proof of advocacy (an introduction to the budget owner, an internal business case, urgency) and confirmation of his seniority and access, since no opportunity contact role records his title. Without exec sponsorship or a second thread, the deal is exposed if he is overruled or deprioritized.

competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any field, task, next step, or call this sweep: Competitors__c is blank and no Avoma calls were matched (source: Competitors__c; Avoma opp+account+attendee-email pull returned 0). The competitive status is genuinely unknown, this could be a sole-source direct evaluation or a quiet multi-vendor process where rivals have not yet surfaced in our conversations. No competitor should be assumed. Confirming with Pak Andianto whether MPM Rent is comparing other vendors, so pricing and the wedge are positioned correctly, is the required move.

deal_movement.summary: Young deal created 11 June 2026, reassigned to George John on 23 June, moved Initial Interest to Qualified on 30 June, with a first demo on 26 June that landed well. The live pulse is a rep-side commitment (share pricing) that is outstanding while buyer inbound has gone quiet for ~14 days.

items.change: First meeting and demo held; buyer impressed with intake and Bahasa Indonesia
Stage advanced Initial Interest to Qualified; typed as New Business

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is documented from any source this sweep (Competitors__c blank, no calls matched). It is unknown whether this is a sole-source direct evaluation or a quiet multi-vendor process. The demo enthusiasm for intake and Bahasa Indonesia is a positive wedge, but with no competitive context the differentiation is unanchored; confirming the field with the buyer is the priority before pricing.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is early but positive on the two criteria the buyer surfaced at the demo: intake capability and Bahasa Indonesia support both landed well. The broader suite and AI story are in scope but not yet validated against a documented requirement set.

best_practice_check.summary: Early Qualified $150k deal riding on one mid-level procurement contact and no competitive context. The win levers now are widening the thread to the budget owner, confirming who we are up against, and anchoring the AI intake value story.
flags: Multi-thread past Pak Andianto to the budget owner before pricing hits an unknown approval chain.
Confirm with the buyer whether MPM Rent is sole-sourcing or comparing vendors, so pricing and the wedge are positioned right.
Anchor the value story on AI intake, Bahasa Indonesia, and Merlin (AIS 7.3 Strong) and tie it to a quantified benefit.

champion_strength.summary: Pak Andianto is a developing champion. He hosted and reacted well to the 26 June demo and pushed the deal forward by asking for pricing for internal approvals, showing genuine product intent. What is still missing is demonstrated advocacy (an intro to the budget owner, an internal business case, urgency) and confirmation of his authority, since no contact role records his seniority. Without a second thread or exec sponsor, he is a single point of failure.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AIS is 7.3 Strong and the demo enthusiasm centered on AI-assisted intake and Bahasa Indonesia, so the AI story is landing at a surface level. Merlin is in scope but the deeper agentic / intake-to-order narrative has not yet been positioned to a senior audience; carrying it into the pricing/value conversation would raise positioning further.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 7.3 (Strong): strong digital-transformation maturity and procurement complexity, but AI investment currently prioritizes operational efficiency over procurement transformation (likelihood to invest in AI procurement 6.8). The 26 June demo confirmed appetite for AI-assisted intake and local-language capture, so the field read is corroborated, not over-rated.

items.detail: Economic buyer unmapped; only a mid-level procurement contact engaged and no senior sponsor identified.

$150k requires internal approval and the approver is unknown; pricing may stall in an undefined approval loop.
No buyer-stated decision date or compelling event; the 31 Dec close is our placeholder, not a buyer commitment.

critical_signals.text: The 26 June demo landed well: the buyer was impressed with intake and how Bahasa Indonesia can be used for request capture, and asked for pricing to take internally.
High-level pricing was promised after the demo but has not been delivered, and it is the buyer's stated gate to internal approvals.
Only Pak Andianto and his procurement team are engaged; the person who must approve the $150k has not been identified or met.
No rival has surfaced in any conversation or field, so it is unclear whether MPM Rent is buying sole-source or quietly comparing vendors.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Early Qualified $150k intake and eProcurement play at MPM Rent: the 26 June demo landed on intake and Bahasa Indonesia, and the one thing between us and momentum is getting the promised pricing to Pak Andianto while we still have no mapped budget owner and no read on the competition.

win_position.text: Genuinely early but real: a first demo on 26 June landed well on intake and Bahasa Indonesia and Pak Andianto asked for pricing to take to internal approvals, though no budget owner is mapped and no competitor or decision timeline is known (source: Next_Step__c, 26 June 2026).
The economic buyer is unmapped: Pak Andianto needing pricing 'for internal approvals' means he does not control the $150k decision himself (source: Next_Step__c, 26 June 2026).
Single-threaded on one mid-level procurement contact with no senior sponsor; the deal is exposed if he is overruled (source: OpportunityContactRole empty; Next_Step__c, 26 June 2026).

The promised high-level pricing has not gone out and opportunity-health flags record no buyer inbound in 14 days, so momentum is thinning (source: SF tasks, 30 June 2026).

No buyer-stated decision date, no confirmed approval chain, and no compelling event; commitment so far is verbal product interest only (source: no decision-process evidence in any field or call).
